lines: Damaris looks over the nave and counts under her breath.
"You said eight dead above. I believe you, but my hands keep arguing."

choices.label: Tell her the chapel is quiet now

lines: Damaris has wrapped her sleeves tight around both wrists.
"When the cult came, I counted eight voices, though the crying children and stone echoes may have hidden more."
"If you go back up, count for me. I need a number that stays still."
"Two laughed. One prayed. One sounded younger than the rest and kept asking where the women were."
"One rebuked another for saying cult: 'We are the Choir of the Open Wound.' I counted every word because they frightened me."
"Polite cruelty wears a clean shirt. I remember the collar."
"Fear opens the ears, though I do not recommend it as a talent."
"If you can choose one, choose singing; people give singers more bread."
"Badly enough that counting treated everyone kinder and never asked for more bread afterward."
"Children, jars, steps, breaths. Anything that proves the next moment came."
"If I count bread, Susanna calls it useful. If I count ghosts, everyone looks at my hands."
"Maybe. Or it gave my fear a little fence to pace inside."
"Either way, I am still here. I suppose a fence does not have to be pretty."
"People lie with numbers. Numbers mostly sit there and get blamed."
"Still, if you find a ninth body, tell me plain. I am tired of being surprised by arithmetic."
"Quiet has breeds: children asleep, snow on tin, a knife held behind a door."
"Which kind is it now?"
"Then I will take it. I have become less proud about the kind of quiet I accept."
